mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free way to run Kubernetes without managing the underlying hardware. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed solutions and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -O https://github.com/linode/linode-cli/releases/download/v1.25.0/linode-cli-1.25.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf linode-cli-1.25.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv linode-cli /usr/local/bin/

linode-cli configure

```

> Download the Linode CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Configure the CLI with your Linode API token.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-east

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with your newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to write to ~/.kube/config.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/linode/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/master/monitoring.yaml

```

> This installs the Cluster Autoscaler.
> This installs monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Linode CLI from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This will remove the CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node versions.
> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with your kubeconfig file or cluster certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate your kubeconfig using 'linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if no resources have been created in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have deployed applications or check other namespaces using 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust resource requests for your deployments.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to incorrect image name or lack of access to the image repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure your cluster has access to the image repository.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Linode regions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Linode account and API token ready.
